howItWorks.intro: This health insurance calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Out-of-Pocket = min(Deductible + (Costs - Deductible) × Coinsurance%, OOP Maximum)
howItWorks.outro: Insurance rates depend on many individual factors. Get personalized quotes from licensed providers.

faq.question: How do I choose the right health plan?
faq.answer: Estimate your expected medical usage, then compare total cost (premiums + out-of-pocket) for each plan tier. Bronze is cheapest if healthy, Gold/Platinum better for frequent care.

faq.question: What is coinsurance?
faq.answer: After meeting your deductible, coinsurance is the percentage of costs you share. With 20% coinsurance, you pay 20% and insurance pays 80% until you hit the out-of-pocket maximum.

faq.question: What is the out-of-pocket maximum?
faq.answer: The most you pay for covered services in a year. For 2026, marketplace plan maximums cannot exceed $9,200 (individual) or $18,400 (family). After hitting it, insurance pays 100%.

faq.question: Are ACA subsidies available?
faq.answer: Premium tax credits are available to marketplace enrollees with income between 100-400% of the federal poverty level. Many people with higher incomes also qualify for reduced premiums.

faq.question: Is the Health Insurance Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Health Insurance Calculator?
sections.body: The Health Insurance Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the insurance planning space. By entering your monthly premium, annual deductible, coinsurance, you get instant results including annual premiums, out-of-pocket costs, total annual cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting annual premiums right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In insurance planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to choose the right coverage with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your monthly premium and need to find the right annual premiums. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Monthly Premium ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your monthly premium — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: annual deductible, coinsurance, out-of-pocket maximum, expected annual medical costs. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your annual premiums, out-of-pocket costs, total annual cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your monthly premium before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both annual premiums and out-of-pocket costs — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to choose the right coverage.
If you're unsure about your expected annual medical costs, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
